mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent above-target inflation (CPIAUCSL > 240) signals central bank hawkishness, prompting rate hikes that depress equity valuations and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or CPIAUCSL's predictive power in regression models drops below 0.15 R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > 150k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals robust labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: Jointly triggered instances show SPX 20-day returns below 0.5% or interaction term significance drops below p < 0.10 in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts (HOUST > 1.2M) combined with expanding GDP (GDPC1 > 2% q/q) indicates strong private sector demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in co-occurring HOUST/GDPC1 spikes fall below 1.0% or interaction term coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 105) reflects expanding manufacturing capacity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days via improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: 5-day returns following INDPRO spikes show negative or insignificant coefficients (p > 0.10) in rolling window regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) signals labor market tightness that drives wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in low-unemployment regimes exceed 1.2% monthly volatility-adjusted returns, or UNRATE's predictive power drops below 0.10 R-squared.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real interest rates (FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL) indicate accommodative monetary policy, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in negative real rate environments show negative or insignificant coefficients (p > 0.05) or interaction term significance drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer spending (RSAFS > 450B) during low-interest-rate environments (FEDFUNDS < 1%) drives corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns in co-occurring RSAFS/FEDFUNDS spikes fall below 0.3% or interaction term coefficient becomes non-significant (p > 0.05).
